subject: [PATCH v1 7/9] fuse: add io-uring kernel-managed buffer ring

2026-03-24.patch_summary: This patch adds support for a kernel-managed buffer ring to the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) io-uring interface. The buffer ring allows for more efficient communication between the server and the kernel by eliminating the need for pinning/unpinning user pages and translating virtual addresses, and also reduces memory usage per queue. The feature is enabled on a per-queue basis and requires preregistration of a kernel-managed buffer ring and a memory region to hold the headers. This patch has two benefits: it eliminates overhead in server-kernel interactions and reduces memory needed for buffers per queue.
